# Call for entries: High-growth companies Asia-Pacific 2027

Register now to be considered for our latest list of the region’s fastest-growing businesses

* [Call for entries: High-growth companies Asia-Pacific 2027 on x (opens in a new window)](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https://www.ft.com/content/e27443f7-4cc9-4c06-a22a-b239896c2b7c&text=Call%20for%20entries%3A%20High-growth%20companies%20Asia-Pacific%202027&via=ft)

[Jump to comments section](#comments-anchor "Jump to comments section")

Stay informed with free updates

Simply sign up to the Asia-Pacific companies myFT Digest -- delivered directly to your inbox.

Asia’s economies are among the fastest-growing in the world, but behind that expansion lie millions of businesses, large and small, that are making progress despite sharp fluctuations in global markets, politics and tariffs.

For the past eight years, the Financial Times and its research partner Statista have ranked high-growth companies in the Asia-Pacific region. From an Australia-based [ticketing platform](https://www.ft.com/content/411b421d-f4cf-4ffe-b0b8-36c934be3cbb?syn-25a6b1a6=1) to [winemakers in India](https://www.ft.com/content/cfe96257-e795-460a-96c8-1cee716d4925?syn-25a6b1a6=1), we have highlighted those organisations that have pressed ahead with their business plans and made them work.

### Register for our list of high-growth companies Asia-Pacific 2027

If your company has a record of revenue growth between 2022 and 2025, click here to [fill in our online form](https://engage.statista-research.com/index.php?r=survey/index&sid=122286&lang=en) to be considered.

Last year, Malaysian ecommerce technology platform Borong had the [highest ranking](https://www.ft.com/content/c5a3ba72-0333-4830-8280-6bd1592e1f85), followed by South Korean legal and accounting group Bznav.

### How to nominate a company

This year, for the ninth time, we are asking for submissions. We will aim to identify those Asia-Pacific businesses with the strongest revenue growth between 2022 and 2025.

All you need to do is fill in [this simple form](https://engage.statista-research.com/index.php?r=survey/index&sid=122286&lang=en).

We will publish the list of companies in March next year, followed in April by a report highlighting some of the most interesting businesses.

### Eligibility

To be eligible for entry, companies must:

* Have generated revenue of at least $100,000 in 2022\*
* Have generated revenue of at least $1mn in 2025\*
* Revenue growth between 2022 and 2025 should have been primarily organic
* Be independent (ie, not a subsidiary or branch office of another company)
* Be based in one of the following Asia-Pacific locations: Australia, China, Hong Kong, India, Indonesia, Japan, Macau, Malaysia, New Zealand, Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, Taiwan, Thailand or Vietnam

*\*Or average currency value equivalent over the course of the relevant fiscal year*

The closing date for the survey is October 30 2026. More information can be found [here](https://rankings.statista.com/en/fastest-growing-companies/rankings/fastest-growing-companies-asia-pacific/participation//).
